root_cause: Container runtime storage is corrupted and prevents container startup
evidence: Kubelet or runtime reports overlay/rootfs/layer corruption while preparing the container filesystem
Container remains in a waiting startup error state instead of reaching a started state

Dominant corruption error: Error: failed to create containerd task: failed to mount rootfs component at /var/lib/containerd/io.containerd.snapshotter.v1.overlayfs/snapshots/842/fs: structure needs cleaning

causes.message: Kubelet asked the runtime to prepare the container root filesystem
Container runtime storage or root filesystem on the node is corrupted
Container cannot start because the runtime cannot mount or unpack its filesystem

pod:checkout-api-rootfs-corrupt: Pod cannot start because the runtime filesystem backing the container is corrupted

likely_causes: overlayfs or snapshotter data under the container runtime storage path is corrupted
Underlying node disk or filesystem errors damaged container runtime metadata or layers
A node crash or unclean shutdown left containerd or Docker layer state inconsistent
suggested_checks: Inspect kubelet and containerd or CRI-O logs for overlayfs, snapshot, or rootfs corruption messages
Check node dmesg and filesystem health for disk or metadata corruption
Inspect the runtime storage path under /var/lib/containerd or /var/lib/docker on the node
